---
title: "linear regression"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
import yfinance as yf
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
import datetime

# 1. Download dei dati (Greggio WTI)
ticker = "CL=F"
data = yf.download(ticker, start="2020-01-01", end=datetime.date.today().strftime('%Y-%m-%d'))

# 2. Preprocessing rapido
# Creiamo una colonna per i giorni come variabile numerica per la regressione
data['Days'] = range(len(data))
data = data.dropna()

# 3. Preparazione del Modello
X = data[['Days']]
y = data['Close']

X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.2, shuffle=False)

# 4. Training
model = LinearRegression()
model.fit(X_train, y_train)

# 5. Predizione
predictions = model.predict(X_test)

# 6. Visualizzazione
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.plot(data.index, y, label='Prezzo Reale')
plt.plot(data.index[len(X_train):], predictions, label='Predizione (Trend)', color='red')
plt.title(f'Analisi Prezzo Petrolio ({ticker})')
plt.legend()
plt.show()
```
